subcommands.name: aliases
subcommands.description: Edit your command aliases

options.description: Reset your alias configuration

subcommands.name: auth
subcommands.description: Add your auth token to enable Graphite CLI to create and update your PRs on GitHub

options.description: Auth token. Get it from: https://app.graphite.dev/activate

subcommands.name: bottom
subcommands.description: Switch to the first branch from trunk in the current stack

subcommands.name: changelog
subcommands.description: Show the Graphite CLI changelog

subcommands.name: checkout
subcommands.description: Switch to a branch. If no branch is provided, opens an interactive selector

options.description: Include untracked branched in interactive selection

subcommands.name: children
subcommands.description: Show the children of the current branch

subcommands.name: completion
subcommands.description: Set up bash or zsh tab completion

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: Configure the Graphite CLI

subcommands.name: continue
subcommands.description: Continues the most recent Graphite command halted by a merge conflict

options.description: Stage all changes before continuing

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: Create a new branch stacked on top of the current branch and commit staged changes. If no branch name is specified but a commit message is passed, generate a branch name from the commit message

options.description: Commit staged changes on the new branch with this message
Stage all unstaged changes on the new branch with this message
Pick hunks to stage before committing
When true, any existing children of the current branch will become children of the new branch

subcommands.name: dash
subcommands.description: Open the web dashboard

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Delete a branch and its corresponding Graphite metadata

options.description: Delete the branch even if it is not merged or closed

subcommands.name: docs
subcommands.description: Show the Graphite CLI docs

subcommands.name: down
subcommands.description: Switch to the parent of the current branch

options.description: The number of levels to traverse downstack

args.name: steps
args.description: The number of levels to traverse downstack

subcommands.name: feedback
subcommands.description: Commands for providing feedback and debug state

options.description: Print a debug summary of your repository. Useful for creating bug report details
Include a blob of json describing your repo's state to help with debugging. Run 'gt feedback debug-context' to see what would be included

args.name: message
args.description: Post a string directly to the maintainers' Slack so they can drown in praise, factor in your feedback, laugh at your jokes, cry at your insults, or fall victim to Slack injection attacks

subcommands.name: fish
subcommands.description: Set up 'fish' tab completion

subcommands.name: fold
subcommands.description: Fold a branch's changes into its parent, update dependencies of descendants of the new combined branch, and restack

options.description: Keeps the name of the current branch instead of using the name of its parent

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Get branches from trunk to the specified branch from remote, prompting the user to resolve conflicts. If no branch is provided, get downstack from the current branch

options.description: Overwrite all fetched branches with remote source of truth

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Display information about the current branch

options.description: Show the changes made by each commit
Show the diff between this branch and its parent. Takes precedence over patch
Show the PR body, if it exists
Show a diffstat instead of a full diff. Modifies either --patch or --diff. If neither is passed, implies --diff

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize Graphite in this repository by selecting a trunk branch. Can also be used to change the trunk branch of the repository

options.description: Untrack all branches
The name of your trunk branch. If no name is passed, you will be prompted to select one interactively

subcommands.name: log
subcommands.description: Commands that log your stacks

subcommands.name: long
subcommands.description: Display a graph of the commit ancestry of all branches

subcommands.name: short
subcommands.description: Log all stacks tracked by Graphite, arranged to show dependencies

options.description: Use the old short logging style, which runs out of screen real estate more quickly. Other options will not work in classic mode
Print the log upside down. Handy when you have a lot of branches
Include untracked branched in interactive selection
Only show ancestors and descendants of the current branch
Only show this many levels upstack and downstack. Implies --stack

subcommands.name: merge
subcommands.description: Merge the pull requests associated with all branches from trunk to the current branch via Graphite

options.description: Asks for confirmation before merging branches. Prompts for confirmation if the local branches differ from remote, regardless of the value of this flag
Reports the PRs that would be merged and terminates. No branches are merged

subcommands.name: modify
subcommands.description: Modify the current branch by amending its commit or creating a new commit. Automatically restacks descendants. If you have any unstaged changes, you will be asked whether you'd like to stage them

options.description: Stage all changes before committing
Create a new commit instead of amending the current commit. If this branch has no commits, this command always creates a new commit
If passed, open an editor to edit the commit message. When creating a new commit, this flag is ignored
Ignore all other flags and start a git interactive rebase on the commits in this branch
The message for the new or amended commit. If passed, no editor is opened
Pick hunks to stage before committing

subcommands.name: move
subcommands.description: Rebase the current branch onto the target branch and restack all of its descendants. If no branch is passed in, opens an interactive selector

options.description: Branch to move the current branch onto
Branch to move (defaults to current branch)

subcommands.name: parent
subcommands.description: Show the parent of the current branch

subcommands.name: pop
subcommands.description: Delete the current branch but reaint the state of files in the working tree

subcommands.name: pr
subcommands.description: Opens the pull request page for a branch or PR number. If no branch is passed, the current branch's PR is opened

args.name: branch
args.description: A branch name or PR number to open

subcommands.name: rename
subcommands.description: Rename a branch and update metadata referencing it. If no branch name is supplied, you will be prompted for a new branch name. Note that this removes any associated GitHub pull request

options.description: Allow renaming a branch that is already associated with an open GitHub pull request

subcommands.name: reorder
subcommands.description: Reorder branches between trunk and the current branch, restacking all of their descendants. Opens an editor where you can reorder branches by moving around a line corresponding to each branch

subcommands.name: restack
subcommands.description: Ensure the current branch is based on its parent, rebasing if necessary. If conflicts are encountered, you will be prompted to resolve them via an interactive Git rebase

options.description: Which branch to run this command from. Defaults to the current branch
Only restack this branch and its ancestors
Only restack this branch
Only restack this branch and its descendants

subcommands.name: revert
subcommands.description: Create a branch that reverts a commit on the trunk branch. Currently experimental

options.description: Edit the commit message

subcommands.name: split
subcommands.description: Split the current branch into multiple single-commit branches

options.description: Split by commit - slice up the history of this branch
Split by hunk - split into new single-commit branches

subcommands.name: squash
subcommands.description: Squash all commits in the current branch and restack upstack branches

options.description: The updated message for the commit
Modify the existing commit message
Don't modify the existing commit message. Takes precedence over --edit

subcommands.name: submit
subcommands.description: Idempotently force push the current branch to GitHub, creating or updating a pull request.  Opens an interactive prompt that allows you to input pull request metadata. 'gt ss' is a default alias for 'gt submit --stack'

options.description: Always push updates, even if the branch has not changed. Can be helpful for fixing an inconsistent Graphite stack view on Web/GitHub resulting from downtime/a bug
Which branch to run this command from. Defaults to the current branch
Reports the PRs that would be submitted and asks for confirmation before pushing branches and opening/updating PRs. If either of --no-interactive or --dry-run is passed, this flag is ignored
If set, marks PR as draft. If --no-interactive is true, new PRs will be created in draft mode
Reports the PRs that would be submitted and terminates. No branches are pushed and no PRs are opened or updated
Input metadata for all PRs interactively. If neither --edit nor --no-edit is passed, only prompts for new PRs
Force push: overwrites the remote branch with your local branch. Otherwise defaults to --force-with-lease
If set, marks all PRs being submitted as merge when ready, which will let them automatically merge as soon as all merge requirements are met
Don't edit PR fields inline. Takes precedence over --edit
If set, publishes PR. If --no-interactive is true, new PRs will be created in draft mode
Rerequest review from current reviewers
Restack branches before submitting. If there are conflicts, output the branch names that could not be restacked
If set without an argument, prompt to manually set reviewers. Alternatively, accepts a comma separated string of reviewers
Submit descendants of the current branch in addition to its ancestors
Only update the PRs that have been already been submitted

subcommands.name: sync
subcommands.description: Pull the trunk branch from remote and prompt to delete any branches that have been merged. Restacks all branches in your repository that can be restacked without conflicts. If trunk cannot be fast-forwarded to match remote, overwrites trunk with the remote version

options.description: Delete merged branches (true by default; skip with --no-delete)
Don't prompt for confirmation before deleting a branch or overwriting trunk to match remote if it can't be fast-forwarded
Pull the trunk branch from remote (true by default; skip with --no-pull)
Restack any branches that can be restacked without conflicts (true by default; skip with --no-restack)
Show progress through merged branches

subcommands.name: top
subcommands.description: Switch to the tip branch of the current stack. Prompts if ambiguous

subcommands.name: track
subcommands.description: Start tracking the current (or provided) branch with Graphite by selecting its parent. This command can also be used to fix corrupted Graphite metadata

options.description: The tracked branch's parent. If unset, prompts for a parent branch
Sets the parent to the most recent tracked ancestor of the branch being tracked. Takes precedence over `--parent`

subcommands.name: trunk
subcommands.description: Switch to the trunk branch

subcommands.name: untrack
subcommands.description: Stop tracking a branch with Graphite. If the branch has children, they will also be untracked. Default to the current branch if none is passed in

options.description: Will not prompt for confirmation before untracking a branch with children

subcommands.name: up
subcommands.description: Switch to the child of the current branch. Prompts if ambiguous

options.description: The number of levels to traverse upstack

args.name: steps
args.description: The number of levels to traverse upstack

options.description: Show Graphite CLI version
Show help for Graphite CLI
Prompt the user. Disable with --no-interactive
Run non interactive
Minimize output to the terminal. Implies --no-interactive
Run git hooks. Disable with --no-verify
Disable git hooks
Display debug output
Working directory in which to perform operations
